mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the effective federal funds rate above 0.25 percent signals the onset of monetary tightening, raising discount rates and reducing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are non-negative in greater than 50 percent of triggered episodes over a 3-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in real retail sales and subdued consumer price inflation indicates healthy demand expansion without cost pressures, lifting corporate margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change positive and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change below 1.0%
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in fewer than 55 percent of joint-signal instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where the unemployment rate has fallen below its natural rate, a subsequent rise in the unemployment rate signals impending demand contraction and earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative returns are not realized in greater than 60 percent of triggered episodes during late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in the industrial production index reflects capacity utilization gains and manufacturing orders, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are not positive in greater than 55 percent of trigger events during a 2-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth above 2.5 percent annualized signals robust aggregate demand that supports top-line revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative or insignificantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) during high-growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held near zero while consumer prices are stable, real policy rates remain deeply negative, forcing capital into risk assets and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 45 percent of joint-signal observations over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in housing starts signal durable demand for construction, related credit expansion, and household formation, which transmits into broad equity market gains over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between HOUST momentum and 20-day SPX returns is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
